title: Mapouria remota
taxonomy: Mapouria remota is classified as a species of plant in the family Annonaceae. This family of plants is commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. The Annonaceae family consists of approximately 196 genera and over 2,000 species of flowering plants. Mapouria remota is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Its classification as a species within the Annonaceae family reflects its botanical relationships and evolutionary history.
uses: Mapouria remota, a plant native to the Amazon rainforest, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Research has shown that it may exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which could be beneficial in treating various health conditions.
